arch_source: device query torch.cuda.get_device_name = NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090; 170 SMs; HBM peak 1.79 TB/s (gemv_bw_30b.py constant)

dequant_bit_exact_equiv.method: Z3 BitVec32 over all 256 bytes x 4 strides (or_preserves_code) + exhaustive 4-code struct bitcast value-equivalence
dequant_bit_exact_equiv.empirical_confirmation: ignore/mortar_decode_parity.py vs original decode: bit_exact=True, maxd=0.000e+00 (gate_up & down)

decode_cost_breakdown_floor_probe.note: in-place w=const probe: mortar gate_up 43.62->23.87 (-45%) = decode is ~45% of wall-clock; magic recovers SHR/AND-I2F portion (~4-5us); the 15us FMA floor is the irreducible dot product

end_to_end_30b_tok_s.verdict: overlapping distributions; end-to-end is HBM-bandwidth-bound (51% peak, 17.97 GB/tok weight stream) so the per-kernel compute win does not surface in the aggregate tok/s

occupancy_lever.note: BO 32->16->8 HURTS (grid + partial-sum overhead); num_warps 8 slower; eager-reduce small-accumulator wash. The dependent-latency stall is not closeable by occupancy knobs at this carved-BCSR layout.

not_verified: ncu occupancy/SM% delta (admin-gated; orchestrator must confirm the per-kernel compute-bound -> the floor-probe shows headroom but isolated-kernel ncu pass owed)
real-carve weights at 30B (random-init systems-only; no quality claim)
B>1 batched decode paths (only B=1 measured)
